overview.location: 3 Brewster Rd, Newark, NJ 07114 — straddles Newark and Elizabeth, NJ, across Essex and Union counties. 9 miles west-southwest of Manhattan, 4.5 miles south of downtown Newark.
overview.passengerVolume: 49.1 million passengers in 2023 (record year). 48.9 million in 2024 (14th busiest in U.S.). Approximately 47 million in 2025.
overview.distinction: First major commercial airport in the New York metropolitan area (opened 1928). Second-busiest airport in the NY metro system behind JFK, ahead of LaGuardia. Fourth-largest United Airlines hub. United Airlines accounts for approximately 68% of all traffic.
overview.operator: Port Authority of New York and New Jersey (leased from the cities of Newark and Elizabeth)
overview.runwayNote: Parallel runways 4L and 4R are only 950 feet apart — the fourth-smallest separation of any major U.S. airport. Runway 4L/22R primarily used for takeoffs, 4R/22L primarily for landings. Runway 11/29 used by smaller aircraft or during strong crosswinds.
overview.founded: October 1, 1928 (originally Newark Metropolitan Airport, later Newark Airport, renamed Newark Liberty International Airport on September 26, 2002 in memory of 9/11 victims)
overview.faaCapacityLimits: FAA imposed temporary flight limits effective May 2025: weekdays 34 arrivals + 34 departures per hour, weekends 28 + 28 per hour (through end of 2025, may be extended).
overview.airTrafficControlNote: Newark airspace control transferred from New York TRACON to Philadelphia TRACON in 2024 due to persistent staffing shortages at NY TRACON.

terminals.name: Terminal A (New Terminal A)
terminals.status: Active — $2.7 billion replacement terminal opened January 12, 2023. All 33 gates operational. Named world's best new airport terminal by Skytrax in 2024, 5-star Skytrax rating, and UNESCO Prix Versailles award for exterior design.
terminals.gates: 33 gates across 3 concourses (A1, A2, A3), all served by 1 central security checkpoint — passengers can move freely between concourses without reclearing security.
terminals.notes: Brand-new state-of-the-art terminal designed by Grimshaw Architects. Light-filled concourses, 60% more seating near gates vs old terminal. Automated boarding pass scanning at security entrance. Millimeter wave scanners and advanced baggage screening. Free WiFi with Wi-Fi 6 throughout. United Club, American Airlines Admirals Club, Delta Sky Club. New American Express Centurion Lounge opening in 2026. Future expansion planned for additional gates.

terminals.name: Terminal B
terminals.status: Active — EWR's main international terminal. 3 concourses (B1, B2, B3) with approximately 25 gates. Earmarked for full demolition and replacement via public-private partnership under the Port Authority's 2026-2035 capital plan.
terminals.gates: Concourse B1: ~10 gates (B40-B49). Concourse B2: ~6 gates (B51-B57). Concourse B3: ~9 gates (B60-B68).
terminals.notes: IMPORTANT: Separate security checkpoints at each concourse — passengers CANNOT move freely between B1, B2, and B3 without reclearing security. Oldest terminal at EWR. International arrivals processing. Delta Sky Club in B1 Connector. Newly redesigned outdoor dog park with grassy turf, bone-shaped rock garden, and hydrant. Terminal B is slated for full replacement; procurement expected to begin 2026-2027.

terminals.name: Terminal C (United Airlines)
terminals.status: Active — United Airlines exclusive terminal. Approximately 60-68 gates across 3 concourses. Heavily modernized with tablet-based ordering, digital wayfinding, and premium lounge facilities.
terminals.gates: Concourse C1: Gates C70-C99. Concourse C2: Gates C101-C115. Concourse C3: Gates C120-C139.
terminals.notes: Three security checkpoints lead into a shared central area — passengers CAN move freely between all three C concourses without reclearing security. Home to the United Polaris Lounge (27,000 sq ft, second largest Polaris Lounge). Multiple United Clubs. Extensive dining and retail. United's domestic and international operations.

transit: AirTrain Newark (monorail connecting terminals + NJ Transit rail station)
NJ Transit North Jersey Coast Line
Amtrak Northeast Regional / Keystone Service (via Newark Liberty Airport Station)
PATH (via NJ Transit to Newark Penn Station)
authority: Port Authority of New York and New Jersey

airTrain.currentSystem: 1996 monorail (being replaced by new $3.5B cable-driven system, construction started October 2025, opening 2030)
airTrain.fare: Free for inter-terminal travel and P4 parking
airTrain.accessFeeNote: Required only at RailLink station (for NJ Transit/Amtrak connection). Raised from $8.50 to $8.75 on March 4, 2026.
paymentMethods: Included in NJ Transit mobile app ticket purchase
airTrain.construction2026: IMPORTANT: Starting January 15, 2026, AirTrain service is suspended on weekdays from 5:00 AM to 3:00 PM for replacement project construction. Free shuttle buses replace AirTrain during closures, with temporary additional stop at rental car facility outside Terminal A. Additional full closures planned September 30 - October 30, 2026 and October 30, 2026 - January 15, 2027.

replacementProject.constructionContract: $1.2 billion for stations and elevated tracks

airTrain.competitorNote: AirTrain access fee is $8.75/person one-way from the NJ Transit rail station. A couple pays $17.50 round-trip in AirTrain fees alone — on top of NJ Transit fares. Off-site parking lots with direct terminal shuttles eliminate this cost entirely.

njTransitTrain.totalWithAirTrain: $15.25-$17.50 per person one-way (train fare + $8.75 AirTrain access fee)
njTransitTrain.frequency: 6+ trains per hour (6 AM - 9 PM weekdays), 4 trains per hour (9 PM - midnight)
njTransitTrain.connections: Newark Penn Station (PATH, Amtrak, NJ Transit local trains and buses)
njTransitTrain.note: AirTrain access fee ($8.75) included when purchasing ticket through NJ Transit Mobile App or ticket vending machines.

amtrak.stationName: Newark Liberty International Airport Station (same as NJ Transit)
amtrak.alsoAvailable: Newark Penn Station (5-minute NJ Transit ride) connects to full Amtrak Northeast Corridor (Acela, Northeast Regional to Boston, Washington DC, Philadelphia)

path.connection: No direct PATH service to airport. Transfer required: Take NJ Transit from EWR Airport Station to Newark Penn Station (~5 minutes), then connect to PATH.
path.pathDestinations: Hoboken, Jersey City (Journal Square, Exchange Place, Grove Street), World Trade Center (Lower Manhattan), 33rd Street (Midtown)
path.futureStudy: Port Authority studying direct PATH extension to airport

routes.route62: Newark Penn Station - Airport - Elizabeth (frequent service)
routes.route67: Brick - Lakewood - Freehold - Jersey City - Newark - Airport
routes.route107: Serves Terminal A and airport area

njTransitBus.note: Buses stop at all three terminals. Route 62 most frequent for local connections.

expressBus.name: Newark Airport Express (formerly CoachUSA / GO Airlink)
expressBus.route: EWR to/from Grand Central Terminal, Bryant Park, Port Authority Bus Terminal (NYC)

taxi.tolls: NJ Turnpike and tunnel/bridge tolls added on top of fare
taxi.note: Unlike JFK, there is NO flat rate from EWR to Manhattan. Fares are fully metered. Surcharges may apply during peak hours.

pickupLocations.terminalA: Arrivals level (lower level), Ride App Pick Up Zones 9 & 10
pickupLocations.terminalB: Standard pickup area at arrivals level
pickupLocations.terminalC: Standard pickup area at arrivals level

rideshare.dropOff: Departures level (upper level) at all terminals
rideshare.surgeFrequency: Common during rush hours, holidays, and bad weather
rideshare.notes: No flat rate from EWR. Upfront pricing shown in app. Can be cheaper than taxi during off-peak but highly unpredictable during rush hours.

rentalCars.location: Consolidated Rental Car Center in P3 Parking Garage, accessible via free AirTrain
rentalCars.airTrainNote: Free AirTrain ride to P3 station. During AirTrain construction closures (2026), free shuttle buses serve as replacement.

helicopter.note: By-the-seat helicopter service. Book at blade.com.

parkingRates: On-airport: P1 (Terminal A) $65/day, P2 (Terminal B) $70/day, P3 (Terminal C) $70/day, P4 Daily Garage $60/day, P6 Economy Lot $35/day drive-up ($21 pre-booked). Cell Phone Lot: Free. Off-site: from ~$6.95/day (Apex, pre-booked). Pre-booking recommended — saves 28-36% vs drive-up rates.

p1ShortTerm.rate: $5.25 per 30 minutes. After 3 hours: $10.50 per 30 minutes.
p1ShortTerm.notes: Attached to Terminal A. Most convenient for short stays and pickups. Expensive for long-term parking.

p2ShortTerm.rate: $5.25 per 30 minutes. After 3 hours: $10.50 per 30 minutes.

p3ShortTerm.rate: $5.25 per 30 minutes. After 3 hours: $10.50 per 30 minutes.
p3ShortTerm.notes: Attached to Terminal C. Also houses consolidated Rental Car Center.

p4DailyParking.rate: $4.25 per 30 minutes. After 3 hours: $8.50 per 30 minutes.
p4DailyParking.connection: Free AirTrain to all terminals. Transit times: ~7 min to Terminal C, ~11 min to Terminal B, ~20 min to Terminal A.
p4DailyParking.evCharging: 13 ChargePoint Level 2 stations on ground level, free electricity
p4DailyParking.notes: Best on-airport option for multi-day stays. Pre-booking strongly recommended. AirTrain closures in 2026 may require shuttle bus connection.

p6EconomyLot.rate: $35 for first 24 hours, then $17.50 per 12-hour period
p6EconomyLot.shuttle: Free 24/7 shuttle to all terminals, departing every 5-10 minutes
p6EconomyLot.notes: Cheapest on-airport option. Fills quickly during holidays and peak travel. Pre-book for guaranteed space and lower rate.

cellPhoneLot.address: 3 Brewster Rd, Newark, NJ 07114 (near P4 garage)
cellPhoneLot.rules: Driver must remain with vehicle. For pickup of arriving passengers only. Wait until your passenger has collected bags and is curbside.
cellPhoneLot.evCharging: ZAPP EV fast charging hub located at cell phone lot — available for airport users and for-hire vehicle drivers. Capacity doubled in September 2025.

onAirportParking.parkingInquiries: Call the Port Authority parking customer service line
onAirportParking.paymentMethods: Credit cards, debit cards, contactless payment. Pre-booking available online.

shuttleInfo: Most off-site lots run free shuttles every 5-15 minutes, 24/7. AirTrain connects terminals and P4 (free for inter-terminal travel). P6 Economy Lot shuttle every 5-10 minutes. During 2026 AirTrain construction closures, free shuttle buses replace monorail service. Off-site lots with direct terminal shuttles eliminate the $8.75/person AirTrain access fee required for NJ Transit rail connections.

parkingLots.name: E-Z Way Parking (EWR)
parkingLots.shuttleInfo: Free 24/7 shuttle to all terminals, 5-10 minute ride without traffic. Five shuttle vans in fleet.
parkingLots.overview: Closest off-site parking lot to Newark Airport entrance. 24/7 valet service with luggage assistance. Fenced, lit, and monitored with video surveillance. One-time $6.00 airport access/service fee (plus tax) on top of daily rate. Silent Passenger fleet tracking for real-time shuttle info. 2-hour free extension if running late.
parkingLots.notes: Closest lot to EWR entrance. $6 one-time service fee added. Valet only — must leave keys. Promo code 25PARK is widely used.

parkingLots.name: ABC Airport Parking (EWR)
parkingLots.shuttleInfo: Free shuttle every 10 minutes to all terminals (A, B, C). 24/7 operation.
parkingLots.overview: Budget-friendly off-airport valet parking less than 2 miles from EWR. Well-lit, fenced lot with 24-hour security. $6.00 one-time service fee (plus tax). Shuttle directly to all terminals.
knownCoupons: Check abcairportparking.net for current 48% off coupon
parkingLots.notes: Good value with frequent shuttle. $6 service fee on top of daily rate. One of the more affordable options near EWR.

parkingLots.name: Apex EWR Airport Parking
parkingLots.shuttleInfo: Round-the-clock shuttle every 5-10 minutes to all EWR terminals.
parkingLots.overview: One of the cheapest off-airport options near Newark. Located just 0.3 miles from the airport entrance. Valet service with 24/7 shuttle. Pre-booking recommended for lowest rates.
parkingLots.notes: Extremely close to airport. Among the lowest daily rates when pre-booked. Valet only — must leave keys.

parkingLots.name: Victoria Parking (EWR)
parkingLots.shuttleInfo: Free shuttle every 10 minutes, 24/7/365 to all terminals.
parkingLots.overview: Offers both indoor ($12/day) and outdoor ($10/day) parking. 1.8 miles from EWR. 24/7 operation with frequent shuttle service. Fenced and monitored facility.
parkingLots.notes: One of few lots offering indoor/covered parking. Indoor rate higher than outdoor. Shuttle is frequent and reliable.

parkingLots.name: Newark Airport Long Term Parking (NALTP)
parkingLots.shuttleInfo: Free 24/7 shuttle, 2-3 minute ride to terminals.
parkingLots.overview: Established off-airport parking facility minutes from EWR. Fenced, patrolled, and under video surveillance. EV charging stations available. NALTP Club is free to join with no annual fee. Not the cheapest, but known for customer service. Accessible via NJ Turnpike Exit 14.
parkingLots.address: NJ Turnpike Exit 14 area (see website for exact address)
knownCoupons: NALTP Club members get exclusive deals
parkingLots.notes: Higher priced than competitors but emphasizes service quality. EV charging available. Dynamic pricing — rates vary by season and occupancy.

parkingLots.name: Hilton Newark Airport Parking
parkingLots.shuttleInfo: Free shuttle every 15 minutes, rides under 10 minutes to terminal.
parkingLots.overview: Hotel parking lot at the Hilton Newark Airport. No hotel stay required. Located 1 mile from terminals. Self-park in uncovered lot with hotel security.
parkingLots.notes: Hotel lot — convenient if combining with Park-Sleep-Fly package. Self-park (keep your keys). Shuttle every 15 min.

parkingLots.name: Courtyard by Marriott Newark Airport Parking
parkingLots.overview: Budget-friendly hotel parking near EWR. No hotel stay required. One of the more affordable options. Self-park lot with hotel amenities accessible.
parkingLots.notes: Among cheapest hotel-based parking options. Good for budget travelers who want a reliable lot.

parkingLots.name: The Parking Point (EWR)
parkingLots.shuttleInfo: Free 24/7 shuttle every 15 minutes. Real-time tracking via Silent Passenger system.
parkingLots.overview: Located approximately 2 miles from EWR. Secure, fenced lot with digital surveillance. Vehicles never leave the fenced area without signed permission. 2-hour free extension available.
knownCoupons: Pre-book online from $5.95/day — significant savings vs walk-up
parkingLots.notes: Excellent pre-book savings ($5.95 vs $9.95 walk-up). Real-time shuttle tracking is a plus. Valet only — must leave keys.

general.discount: Friends & family rate ($17/day at EZ Way)

tips: Pre-book online to save 28-36% vs drive-up rates at official lots
Off-site lots start as low as $3.35/day via Way.com or $5.95/day via The Parking Point
Check Groupon for bundled deals on Victoria Parking and other EWR lots
Book through aggregators like Triply, Way.com, SpotHero, or ParkWhiz to compare rates
Prices are dynamic — booking further in advance generally locks in lower rates
NALTP Club (free) offers exclusive member discounts at Newark Airport Long Term Parking
AAA members may get additional discounts at participating lots

cellPhoneLot.location: Cell phone lot off Brewster Road, adjacent to P4
cellPhoneLot.note: Capacity doubled in September 2025. Available to airport users and for-hire vehicle drivers.

evCharging.offSiteLots: NALTP offers EV charging stations. Most other off-site lots do not advertise EV charging. EV drivers should charge before parking or use NALTP/P4.
evCharging.recommendation: P4 garage offers free EV charging but fills up. Arrive early for a charging spot.

tsaPreCheck.avgWaitTime: 5-8 minutes during morning peak (vs 35-50 minutes standard)

operatingHours.terminalA: Sun-Fri 4:30 AM - 8:00 PM, Sat 4:30 AM - 7:00 PM
operatingHours.terminalB: Sun-Fri 4:30 AM - 8:00 PM, Sat 4:30 AM - 7:00 PM

clear.note: CLEAR+ uses biometrics to skip ID check and go directly to TSA physical screening.

standardWaitTimes.peakWait: 45-60 minutes, occasionally 90+ minutes during holiday rushes
standardWaitTimes.tips: Terminal A has a single centralized checkpoint for all 3 concourses. Terminal B has separate checkpoints per concourse — check your gate BEFORE entering security.

arrivalRecommendation.duringAirTrainConstruction: Add 15-30 extra minutes due to shuttle bus replacements for AirTrain service

terminalA.name: United Club
terminalA.access: United Club membership, Star Alliance Gold, United Polaris/Business on United or Star Alliance.

terminalA.name: American Airlines Admirals Club
terminalA.access: Admirals Club membership, Citi AAdvantage Executive Card, oneworld Emerald/Sapphire on AA.

terminalA.name: Delta Sky Club
terminalA.access: Delta Sky Club membership, Delta Reserve Amex, Delta One, SkyTeam Elite Plus.

terminalA.name: American Express Centurion Lounge (opening 2026)
terminalA.access: Amex Platinum, Centurion, Delta Reserve. Opening 2026.
terminalA.note: Brand new lounge coming to the newest terminal.

terminalB.name: Delta Sky Club
terminalB.access: Delta Sky Club membership, Delta Reserve Amex, Delta One, SkyTeam Elite Plus.

terminalB.name: Art & Lounge
terminalB.access: Priority Pass accepted. Day pass available.
terminalB.note: Pre-security location — accessible without a boarding pass.

terminalC.name: United Polaris Lounge
terminalC.access: ONLY for passengers flying United Polaris business class or first/business on long-haul Star Alliance partner flights. NO day passes. NO credit card access. NO United Club membership access.
terminalC.amenities: Full dining, shower suites, day beds, cocktail bar, à la carte menu.

terminalC.name: United Club (multiple locations)
terminalC.location: Terminal C — multiple locations across concourses
terminalC.access: United Club membership, Star Alliance Gold, United Polaris/Business, United Club Infinite Card.

lounges.priorityPassTip: Priority Pass options are limited at EWR. Art & Lounge (Terminal B, pre-security) and Be Relax Spa (Terminal B) accept Priority Pass. No Priority Pass lounges in Terminal A or C.

wifi.cost: Free — no registration, no time limit
wifi.avgSpeed: 20-50 Mbps download (up to 50 Mbps in normal conditions)
wifi.terminalASpeed: Wi-Fi 6 in Terminal A — faster and more reliable than older terminals
wifi.peakCongestionSpeed: Speeds may drop significantly during peak travel times
wifi.loginProcess: Select _FreeEWRWiFi network, login portal appears automatically
wifi.note: Terminal A has the newest WiFi infrastructure (Wi-Fi 6). Terminals B and C use older systems.

luggageStorage.onSite: No luggage storage lockers at Newark Airport (removed for security). No staffed storage counters inside terminals.

offSiteAlternatives.locations: Newark area partner shops and hotels

luggageStorage.note: All off-site services connect travelers with local shops and hotels for secure bag storage near the airport.

indoor: Terminal A — Central Terminal Area (TSA re-composure area), post-security
Terminal B — B3 Satellite (near Gates 65-66), post-security
Terminal B — B1 Connector (next to Delta Sky Club), post-security
outdoor: Terminal A — Level 1 Arrivals North Courtyard (North Side), pre-security
Terminal B — Outside Courtyard (across from Building 125), pre-security
Terminal B — Immigration Hall South Side, pre-security
Terminal C — Level 2, C1 Courtyard, pre-security
petFacilities.dogPark: Terminal B features a newly redesigned outdoor dog park with grassy turf, a bone-shaped rock garden, and a fire hydrant.
petFacilities.features: Signs with international pet relief symbol designate all areas.

peakTravelTimes.bestTimeToFly: Early morning (6-7 AM) or midday (11 AM - 2 PM) for shortest security waits and best on-time performance

arrivalRecommendation.duringAirTrainConstruction: Add 15-30 extra minutes for shuttle bus connections

weatherPatterns.onTimeRate: ~84% of flights arriving within 14 minutes of schedule (mid-2025, best in NYC area). Performance varies significantly by season.
weatherPatterns.historicalReputation: Historically one of the worst airports in the U.S. for delays. Significant improvement in mid-2025 after runway repairs and FAA capacity limits.
weatherPatterns.climate: Humid subtropical/continental. Hot summers, cold winters. All four seasons impact operations.
weatherPatterns.commonDisruptions: Winter nor'easters and snowstorms, summer afternoon thunderstorms, fog, wind shear
weatherPatterns.winterImpact: Major storms can cancel hundreds of flights. Snow and ice require extensive deicing operations. Port Authority has invested in rapid-deicing technologies and expanded snow-removal fleets.
weatherPatterns.summerStorms: Afternoon thunderstorms June-August cause 1-3 hour delays. Ground stops frequently imposed.
weatherPatterns.fogImpact: Morning fog, especially spring and fall, can reduce runway capacity significantly.
weatherPatterns.cascadingEffect: EWR disruptions cascade across the national network — Newark is a major United hub feeding connections nationwide.
weatherPatterns.bestMonths: September-October and April-May typically see fewest weather disruptions
weatherPatterns.runwayClosureHistory: Runway 4L-22R closed April 15 - June 2, 2025 for $121M renovation. Reopened 13 days early. Caused significant delays during closure period.
weatherPatterns.faaCapReduction: FAA temporary flight caps (2025) reduced hourly operations, which paradoxically improved on-time performance by reducing congestion.

construction2026.status: Major multi-billion-dollar redevelopment in progress across multiple projects.
projects: AirTrain Newark Replacement ($3.5B) — New cable-driven system replacing 1996 monorail. Ground broken October 2025. 2.5-mile system with 4 stations. Opening 2030. Weekday service suspensions throughout 2026 with shuttle bus replacements.
Terminal A — COMPLETE (2023). $2.7B new terminal, 33 gates. Future expansion for additional gates planned.

Terminal B Replacement — Port Authority's 2026-2035 capital plan calls for full demolition and rebuild via public-private partnership. Procurement expected to begin 2026-2027. No construction start date announced.
Runway 4L-22R Renovation ($121M) — COMPLETE (June 2, 2025). Reopened 13 days ahead of schedule.
Third Major Taxiway — Proposed in capital plan to reduce flight delays.
New Simplified Roadway Network — Proposed in capital plan.
Community Access Point to Airport Train Station — Construction set for completion in 2026, improving mass transit access for underserved areas of Newark and Elizabeth.
Newark Penn Station to EWR — Study phase for potential PATH extension to the airport.
construction2026.travelImpact: AirTrain weekday suspensions (5 AM - 3 PM) are the primary traveler impact in 2026. Free shuttle buses run during closures. Allow extra time for connections. Terminal B travelers should be aware of future demolition timeline.

airTrainClosures2026.mitigation: Free shuttle buses with stops at all terminals + rental car facility + P4 + RailLink station

parkSleepFly.description: Hotel + parking + shuttle packages near EWR — stay the night before, park free for your trip, shuttle to terminal.
parkSleepFly.priceRange: From ~$105/night (1-night stay + up to 7-14 days parking + shuttle)

popularHotels.name: Newark Liberty International Airport Marriott
popularHotels.parkingPackage: 7-night park & fly package available. Additional parking $38/night after 7 days.
popularHotels.highlight: Only hotel directly on airport property.

popularHotels.name: SpringHill Suites Newark Liberty International Airport
popularHotels.highlight: Suites with microwave, wet bar, mini fridge. Free internet.

popularHotels.name: Hilton Newark Airport
popularHotels.highlight: Also offers parking-only (no hotel stay required).

popularHotels.name: The Kenilworth Hotel - Airport West
popularHotels.highlight: Park, Sleep & Fly package with up to 14 days parking.

popularHotels.name: Fairfield Inn Newark Airport
popularHotels.highlight: Standard airport hotel with reliable service.

keyDistances.toMidtownManhattan: 16 miles, 30-60 min by car (highly variable), ~23 min by NJ Transit train + AirTrain
keyDistances.toLowerManhattan: 13 miles, 25-50 min by car, ~35-45 min via NJ Transit + PATH
keyDistances.competitiveAdvantage: EWR is significantly closer to Manhattan than JFK (16 mi vs 16 mi but faster via NJ Transit train at 23 min vs 60-90 min AirTrain+subway). However, the $8.75 AirTrain access fee + NJ Transit fare totals $15-17/person one-way. Off-site lots with direct terminal shuttles eliminate transit costs entirely.

fromManhattan.note: Tolls collected westbound (NJ to NY) only on bridges/tunnels. Eastbound (NY to NJ) is toll-free. NJ Turnpike tolls additional.

tolls.njTurnpike: Varies by entry/exit. Approximately $1.50-$4.60 E-ZPass within the EWR area.
tolls.taxiTollsNote: All tolls charged on top of metered fare when taking a taxi.

p4DailyPreBooked.airTrainNote: Free AirTrain from P4 to terminals

apexPreBooked.airTrainNote: Direct shuttle to terminal — no AirTrain needed

savingsComparison.keyDifference: Off-site parking at EWR offers dramatic savings. Pre-booked rates from $3.35-$6.95/day vs $21-$65/day on-airport. Direct shuttles eliminate the $8.75/person AirTrain fee that NJ Transit riders pay. For a couple on a 6-day trip, off-site parking ($36-$54) beats NJ Transit round-trip ($61) AND saves the hassle of luggage on trains.

interTerminalTransfers.method: AirTrain (free for inter-terminal travel) — during 2026 construction closures, free shuttle buses
interTerminalTransfers.terminalBNote: Terminal B has separate security at each concourse (B1, B2, B3). If transferring between concourses within Terminal B, you MUST reclear security.
interTerminalTransfers.terminalANote: Terminal A has a single central checkpoint — all 3 concourses accessible without reclearing.
interTerminalTransfers.terminalCNote: Terminal C has shared post-security area across all 3 concourses.
interTerminalTransfers.criticalNote: International arrivals must clear Customs, collect checked bags, and re-check them for connecting flights. You must exit security and reclear at your departure terminal.
interTerminalTransfers.during2026Construction: AirTrain suspended weekdays 5 AM - 3 PM throughout much of 2026. Free shuttle buses connect all terminals, P4, and RailLink station. Allow extra time.

liveSources._description: Authoritative URLs for article generation. Verified 2026-04-03.
